biography:
POV: you just entered the fat loss group chat
-45lbs naturally | 875k+ on YouTube
Trainer, nutritionist + protein pusher @lsfnutrition 
Start here ⬇️

items.caption_text:
I’ll go first… 

For  years I genuinely believed this one and well I would drop pounds at first it never lasted. I had no idea that cutting out carbs was actually making my fat loss long-term way harder! Carbohydrates are absolutely essential for your body to be able to function in a healthy way (especially with my hypothyroidism!) 👇

For women specifically, cutting carbs too low tanks your thyroid hormone T3 which directly slows your metabolism. It disrupts your menstrual cycle and reproductive hormones. It spikes cortisol which tells your body to hold onto fat especially around your midsection. And it kills your performance in the gym which means less muscle built and fewer calories burned.

Carbs are not the enemy. They fuel your workouts, regulate your hormones, support your mood, and actually make fat loss sustainable long term.

The real key is choosing the right ones most of the time. Sweet potatoes, rice, oats, fruit, potatoes. And eating them in amounts that keep you in a deficit.

I eat carbs every single day. I lost 45 pounds, got pregnant lost the baby weight,  I’m 39 years old and the leanest I’ve ever been. 🤍

Save this for the next time someone tells you to cut carbs 👇

items.caption_text:
I want to challenge you to MOVE IN MAY! rting May 1st,commit to walking 60 minutes a day and watch your waistline shrink, body fat drop, and mood completely shift by June… here’s why this works 👇 follow @lovesweatfitness to do it with me!

60 minutes of walking burns roughly 300-400 calories depending on your pace and bodyweight. Over 30 days that’s 9,000-12,000 extra calories burned. That’s 2-3lbs of pure fat gone without changing a single thing about your diet or workouts.

But the fat loss isn’t even the best part.
Your cortisol drops. Which means less belly bloat, less stress eating, and better sleep. Your mood shifts. 

Walking is one of the most underrated tools for anxiety and mental clarity. There’s a reason every walk ends with you feeling better than when you started. And your body starts looking tighter and more defined even before the scale moves.

You don’t have to do it all at once. 30 minutes in the morning and 30 minutes after dinner. Or three 20 minute walks throughout your day. However it fits your life.
No gym membership. No equipment. No recovery needed. No excuses.

One month. 60 minutes a day. And a version of yourself by June that you genuinely didn’t expect.

Who’s starting May 1st with me? Drop a 🚶‍♀️ below 👇

items.caption_text:
Full calorie & macro totals ➡️

If you feel like staying in a deficit on vacation is impossible, I get it. I used to feel like the second I left home all progress went out the window. But here’s what actually works for me now: I don’t try to be perfect every single day. I stick to my foundations to make the easy days easy so the fun days can actually be fun without the stress. 

this is a mix of stuff we made at the house re-renting and eating out, but even the things we made could easily be ordered at a hotel with some modifications if needed. 

My carbs are pretty low here this day, but that’s on purpose. I know there will be nights with margaritas or a dinner where I’m not tracking anything. So when I can keep carbs lower without it feeling like a sacrifice, I do. That way the week balances out and I’m not playing catch-up.

The one thing that doesn’t change no matter what: protein stays high, it will literally save you hundreds of calories, especially on vacation. Two scoops of protein, chicken, eggs, carnitas for about 129g without it feeling forced. That’s what protects your muscle and keeps you from coming home feeling like you lost all your progress.

You don’t need to be crazy strict on vacation. You just need a loose plan and the ability to scan a menu for the best option. I always focus on protein, veggies, and a small serving of a carb. Get sauces on the side and choose one drinks or dessert… You don’t need them every day and you don’t need both every time. It’s simpler than you think.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Comment BLOAT and I’ll message you with the no bloat protein. I use every day and my Bloat Be Gone digestive enzyme/herb blend that saves my belly especially on vacation.!!!

items.caption_text:
We’re not shopping for our cravings, we’re shopping for our goals. If you’re trying to lose fat and eat healthier, get this right first, worry about calories later. If you stick to these foods 90% of the time I guarantee you will start to drop some fat.

Yes, you can overeat healthy foods, but if you’re prioritizing 1g of protein per lbs of your goal weight and fill in the rest with these fats and carbs, it’s gonna be pretty hard to do.

 I have ideas for breakfast, lunch, and dinner recipes at the end & my high protein in-n-out burger sauce to help you hit your goals👉🏼 

Comment PREP and I’ll send you a message with a link to my easiest meal prep, high protein budget friendly grocery hauls, recipes and weight loss tips videos + save this for your next grocery run! 

And comment  TONED if you want my free ebook with sample meal plan, my workout split & tips to lose fat.

items.caption_text:
How many steps do your walk a day? You’ve probably heard 10,000 steps is the goal for fat loss, but here’s what the research actually shows…

Somewhere between 7,000 and 10,000 steps daily is the sweet spot for most women when it comes to fat loss, metabolic health and longevity.

And here’s the part nobody talks about, but is MAJOR! 

The difference between 3,000 steps and 8,000 steps in daily calorie burn can be 300-400 calories. Over a week that’s 2,100-2,800 calories…make it 10k and girl we are talking almost 1lb of fat loss each week just by adding extra steps! Not cutting more calories. Moving more! Over a month that’s meaningful fat loss without a single extra workout.

Most women already get 2,000-4,000 steps just living their day. So the gap to 8,000 or 10,000 is really only 45-60 minutes of intentional walking.

Break it up into a few quick 10-15minute walks or split it into a 20 minute morning walk and a 30 minute evening walk and it doesn’t even feel like another thing to do. It’s just relaxing time for YOU

Walking is not substitution for the incredible benefits of strength training… you should absolutely be hitting 3 days a week and if you want a simple ola you can do at home download my @lsfmove  app and you can track and log your steps there too 👉🏼 but walking IS one of the most underrated fat loss tools available to you and it costs nothing but a little time.

Start tracking your steps today. Aim for 8,000 minimum and work up from there. Your body will do the rest. 🤍

items.caption_text:
This one label trick quietly ruined half the foods in my pantry 😅 but SOOOOOOO worth it!

If you’ve been grabbing “high protein” this and “high protein” that assuming they’re actually helping you hit your goals, we need to talk.

The add a zero hack is the simplest way I’ve found to figure out if a food is actually a good protein source, or just clever marketing in a pretty label with tons of added calories you don’t need! 

Here’s how it works 👇
Step 1. Look at the grams of protein on the label.
Step 2. Add a zero to the end, basically multiply it by 10.
Step 3. Compare that number to the calories per serving.
Step 4. If the calories are at or below that new number, it’s a solid protein pick. If they’re higher, it’s not really a high protein food, it just has a little protein added to it.
Let me show you what I mean.

Fage 0% Greek yogurt has 17g of protein and 90 calories. 
Add a zero to 17, you get 170. Calories are well under that. Solid pick. ✅

A popular “protein” granola has 10g of protein and 210 calories. Add a zero, you get 100. Calories are more than double that. It’s a carb and sugar bar dressed up as protein. ❌

Most “protein” bars have 15g of protein and 250+ calories. Add a zero, you get 150. Calories are higher. It’s basically a candy bar with a little protein sprinkled in. ❌

LSF @lsfnutrition plant protein has 20-23g protein per serving and 120 calories. No sugar, no grit, no bloating. Just a gold star for hitting your protein goals ✅ 

This one trick has genuinely changed how I grocery shop and it’s one of the fastest ways to stop wasting money and calories on foods that aren’t actually helping you reach your goals.

Save this and use it next time you’re at the store. Your future self will thank you. 🤍

Comment PROTEIN and I’ll send you my favorite no bloat protein powder that actually passes the test 👇

items.caption_text:
And do it NATURALLY🤍 If you’re in a phase of life where your body feels like it’s working against you, struggling to lose weight, stick to habits or just get control of your body… I have felt that exact feeling and you’re so not alone. Your diagnosis or stage in life is not your identity or your destiny…. It is just information. And there is so much within your control.

Here’s what actually works for me, even when my body makes it harder than it should be.

Protein at every single meal. At least 30g per meal. With a thyroid condition your metabolism needs every advantage it can get and protein is your most powerful tool.

Strength training three days a week following my @lsfmove MOVE app. Not six days. Not endless cardio. Three focused strength sessions that progressively challenge me. Plus one  HIIT session max, and walking on the other days. That’s the my routine

A moderate calorie deficit. Non-negotiable. You cannot out-exercise a diet that isn’t supporting fat loss. Calculate your TDEE and eat 300-500 calories below that number. Comment CALORIES and I’ll send you a video breaking down exactly how to do this.

Managing stress and protecting sleep. Cortisol is the silent enemy of fat loss especially with autoimmune and thyroid issues. Seven to eight hours, boundaries around stress, rest without guilt. This is part of the strategy, not a bonus.

Supporting my body daily with (her)FORM™️ @lsfnutrition One unflavored scoop every day in my water has:
5g of Creapure®️creatine for lean muscle and body composition
🫜 betaine that research shows helps women lose nearly DOUBLE the body fat
🌱 L-glutamine for recovery, cravings, and gut health which has been a game changer for my IBS and colitis, and electrolytes so everything actually absorbs.

👉🏼This year alone I dropped 10lbs of fat and gained 5lbs of lean muscle. Postpartum. With hypothyroidism. Three days of strength a week.

You are not the exception to every rule. I thought I was too. I was wrong. 🤍

Comment FORM and I’ll send you the deets about (her)FORM or CALORIES for my deficit calculator video 👇

items.caption_text:
I’m not sitting or walking around  posed 24/7… but I’m also not walking around slouched and disengaged either.

Posture matters. Angles matter. Just being aware of how you’re standing can change everything (and it’s about more than how you look… It impacts how you actually show up mentally too!)

And that’s what you’re seeing on social media most of the time.

People showing up a little more put together, a little more intentional, a little more “their best.”

Not because it’s fake… just because no one is posting their worst angle, bad lighting, or bloated end-of-day moment unless it’s intentional to share something helpful.

So if you ever feel like you don’t measure up, just remember you’re comparing your real life to someone else’s highlight.

Comparing can help inspire, but if you take it to far it will distract you from focusing on what YOU are capable of

 @lovesweatfitness come say hi 🤍

items.caption_text:
I took creatine every single day for 6 months... and I need to share what happened 👇

I’ll be honest, I was kinda scared. thought it would make me puffy, bloated or was just a muscle guy thing…not for me. I had every excuse women have been handed about this supplement for years , especially just not sure which one was quality or worth it.

but I was tired of doing everything right, not feeling 100%  and knowing my body was capable of more.

so I went deeper into the research, built something better, and the results genuinely changed how I feel in my body every single day.

here’s what’s inside (her)FORM™ and why it matters for YOU...

🧬Creapure® creatine, the purest in the world, for strength, lean muscle, and body composition. women store less creatine than men naturally, meaning we have more to gain from it than anyone.

🫜 betaine, for fat loss and muscle gain. in research, women lost nearly double the body fat vs the control group. same workouts. different results.

🌱 L-glutamine, for cravings, bloating, and recovery. one hour of training depletes it by 40%. this is the ingredient that makes you feel like yourself again.

🥥 sea salt + coconut, for cellular hydration. because none of the above works without it.
you deserve to feel strong, energized, and at home in your body. not someday. now.

comment HER and I’ll send you the link 🤍

items.caption_text:
To every woman who’s ever been told “once you have kids you’ll never get your body back”… I need you to hear me…

After losing 45lbs with hypothyroidism, pregnancy honestly scared me. I knew how hard my body worked just to maintain, and I didn’t know what it would do to everything I’d built. But I kept coming back to the same truth… the foundational things that worked then will ALWAYS work…

So I stayed consistent with strength training throughout my entire pregnancy, focused on healing and fueling postpartum, and got back to it as soon as I was cleared. Three days a week. At home. A set of dumbbells and my @lsfmove app workouts.

This year I developed (her)FORM, my daily creatine and recovery blend built for women. One scoop every morning, and it has genuinely changed how I recover and how my body responds. This year alone I dropped 10lbs of body fat and gained 5lbs of lean muscle. Without any significant  changes to my diet or workouts. .

Your body is not broken. It’s not “gone.” It just needs the right support and consistency.

Strong looks good on you💪🏼On all of us. 🤍

Comment FORM if you want to learn more about (her)FORM and I’ll send you the link👇

items.caption_text:
She’s here! 

I lost 45lbs and thought I had it figured out. But this year as a 39yr old toddler mom, something shifted.

I started adding one scoop of this mixture I made every day  into my water or smoothies and in a few months I had dropped 10lbs of body fat and gained 5lbs of lean muscle. Same workouts and clean eating, just one little change. 

Here's what's inside and why it works:
💪 Creapure® Creatine... the purest form in the world. Builds lean muscle, shifts body composition, boosts strength and energy. Women naturally store less creatine than men, which means we have more to gain from it than anyone.

🌿 Betaine... amplifies your creatine AND research showed women taking it lost nearly DOUBLE the body fat vs the control group. Same workouts. Different results. This is INCREDIBLE! 

🔄 L-Glutamine... kills cravings at the cellular level, speeds recovery and helps heal your gut.

🧂 Sea Salt + Coconut... electrolytes so everything actually absorbs.

Why every day? Creatine works by saturation, consistency is the whole point.

Here's what to expect:
Weeks 1-2: energy up, cravings quiet down Weeks 3-4: stronger workouts, faster recovery Weeks 6-9: body composition shifts you can actually see

No taste. No texture. Stirs into anything. One scoop and you're done.

Now (her)FORM is finally here for you to try too! Check here out (link in bio)

items.caption_text:
3 habits that made losing 45 lbs feel a LOT easier

I used to think weight loss had to feel hard all the time. Like I needed more discipline, more willpower, more motivation.

But the biggest shift for me was making it feel simple and repeatable.

These are the 3 habits that changed everything:

1. Protein at every meal (30–40g)
Once I started actually hitting about 130–150g a day, I wasn’t constantly hungry or thinking about food. It made staying in a calorie deficit way easier without feeling restricted.

2. Walking every single day (10–12k steps)
This was the missing piece for me. Not more workouts… more daily movement. It keeps your calorie burn up without stressing your body.

3. Keeping my meals simple
Same foods, same structure. Protein + veggies + a carb.
Less decision making = way more consistency.

That’s it.

Not a crazy diet. Not a new plan every week. Just habits I could stick to over and over again.

That’s what helped me lose 45 lbs… and actually keep it off.

Follow @lovesweatfitness for more simple, realistic weight loss tips.

items.caption_text:
Most of the time, it’s just one SMALL change that will finally get your body working for you again…

If your sleep or recovery is off, if your energy and hormones are all over the place or your not adding enough lean muscle… your body holds onto fat a lot harder.

That’s exactly where I was.

Eating clean, working out, doing everything “right”… and my body just wasn’t changing the same way anymore.

I’m 39, I have a 3 year old, hypothyroidism and colitis… and I could feel something was off.

First, I had to get honest with my food so I could be sure I was in a deficit to actually lose fat (this part is a non-negotiable)

I figured out what a real deficit looked like for me, made sure I was hitting around 130g of protein and tracked long enough to stop guessing.

That’s what got the fat loss started, but THIS 👇🏻 is the part that changed my entire body…

This year I’ve lost almost 10 lbs and leaned out more than I have in a long time… doing the same workouts, eating the same meals, but finally seeing results because of 5 powerful science-backed ingredients I started mixing together in my water every day. 

That’s why I made (her)FORM™. 5-ingredient daily blend to support strength, recovery, hormones, and body composition all in one scoop.

Here’s why it works:

Creatine (Creapure®) helps with strength, lean muscle, energy, and brain function. Most women actually have lower levels, so we benefit more.

Betaine helps with body comp and insulin response. There’s research showing women lost nearly double the body fat adding it in daily.

L-glutamine supports recovery, gut health, and helps calm those random sugar cravings.

And sea salt + coconut help your body actually absorb and use everything.

It’s not a  magic fat loss supplement. It’s the missing support so what you’re already doing finally works.

One scoop a day. That’s it.

Comment HER and I’ll send you a FREE women’s guide to creatine and send you the link to grab it!

items.caption_text:
6 pounds. That’s it. But look at the difference…

I’ve been told my whole career that creatine would make me bloated, hold water, and make me look puffy. So I avoided it for years. YEARS. And honestly? That might be one of the biggest mistakes I made in my fitness journey.

Because what actually happened when I started taking it was the opposite. My body started shifting in ways that had nothing to do with the scale. Less fat. More lean muscle. More definition. More energy in my workouts. Faster recovery. And the cravings that used to derail me started quieting down in a way I couldn’t explain at first until I really understood what the ingredients were actually doing at the cellular level.

That’s why I built (her)FORM™. Because I kept looking for a supplement that was actually designed for women like us. Women in their 30s, 40s and beyond whose bodies are shifting hormonally and not responding the same way they used to. Women who are doing everything right and still feeling like something is off.

Just five ingredients. Creapure® Creatine monohydrate, Betaine, Glutamine, Sea Salt, and Dehydrated Coconut. No fillers. No flavoring. No fluff. Just the things your body actually needs to perform, recover, and feel like herself again.

(her)FORM™ launches April 14th and early access is open right now.

Comment HER below and I’ll send you the link to be the first to know when it drops🤍

Also this new song @jasonderulo 🔥

items.caption_text:
no, it’s not too late. No it doesn’t matter how old you are. These are the same rules that have and will always be the most important when it comes to losing fat at all stages of life.

✔️ track what you put in your body. I know you might not like to, but it is the only way to be sure you’re actually in a calorie deficit so you’re actually able to lose that. You don’t have to do it forever, but the more consistently you do, the more accurate you are, the faster you will lose fat. 

To be fair, I lost 45 pounds without tracking, but especially in my late 30s after having a baby, It became clear that I needed a true baseline to see results.

🥯Eat 1 g protein pound of your goal weight daily. So if you think a healthy weight for your body is 130 pounds, 130 g of protein every single day.. 

👟 Walk daily, ideally aim for 8 to 10,000 steps, but if you’re not tracking, try to get 1-2,  30minute walks in each day or break it up into a bunch of a little 10 minute ones

💪🏼 Lift weights. Yes, especially us women, especially over 30! You should be a strength training at least three days a week to maintain and build lean muscle mass.

💡Stay consistent. Inconsistency crushes more body goals than an occasional hamburger and fries.

items.caption_text:
It doesn’t have to feel so hard.,, I know why it does. I’ve felt it, but I promise it doesn’t need to be. Losing 45lbs , then another 20+ in my mid 30’s after having my son taught me change is in the simple daily habits you just keep going over and over again even when you don’t see the results in the timeline you make up in your head 🤍

If fat loss feels harder than it should… this is why I simplified everything.

Protein. Strength. Steps. Hydration.
But also… supporting my body so it could actually respond.

That’s exactly why I created (her)FORM™

It’s a daily formula to support strength, recovery, and the way your body actually responds to fat loss with 5g of Creapure Creatine Monohydrate 

Because most women don’t realize:

• We store less creatine than men, which means we actually have more to gain from it. Strength, lean muscle, energy, even brain support as hormones shift. ￼

• Betaine helps improve body composition and how your body responds to carbs and training. ￼

• Glutamine supports gut health, recovery, and helps reduce cravings at the source. ￼

• And hydration isn’t just water… you need electrolytes to actually absorb it and reduce that puffy, inflamed feeling. ￼

Simple works. But the right support makes it work better.

Comment HER and I’ll add you to early access before it drops next week 🤍

items.caption_text: Revealing our newest product 👀 coming 4.14

items.caption_text:
Before your drive yourself crazy trying to lose weight, this is the easiest place to start. You don’t need to do everything all at once, but you do need to track something consistently. And if I woke up and needed to lose 45lbs again I’d start by tracking my protein.

Because when I was struggling the most, I was doing what most women do without realizing it… not enough protein, inconsistent  carbs, high fats, snacking more than I realized… and always hungry.

Once I fixed that, everything changed.

Here’s why protein is the best place to start:

It keeps you full so you naturally snack less.
It helps you hold onto muscle so your body actually looks leaner.
It makes it easier to stay in a calorie deficit without feeling miserable.
And it gives you a baseline so you can adjust carbs + fats around it

Most women I talk to are getting maybe 60–80g/day.

If fat loss is your goal, you should be closer to 120–150g/day or 1g per pound of your healthy goal weight 
(about 20–40g per meal).

What that actually looks like:

Breakfast: eggs + egg whites or a protein shake
Lunch: 4–5 oz chicken or turkey
Snack: protein smoothie or Greek yogurt
Dinner: 4–6 oz salmon, beef, or chicken

Simple. Repeatable. Effective.

If you do nothing else… start here.

Comment PROTEIN and I’ll send you the one I use every day to make it super easy to hit my goal! No bloating, no sugar, and it actually tastes REALLY good 🤍

items.caption_text: While everyone’s busy trying to figure out what’s real or fake today (or always now…?) I’ll just be here crushing some serious goals😬  You in?

raw.caption: While everyone’s busy trying to figure out what’s real or fake today (or always now…?) I’ll just be here crushing some serious goals😬  You in?

items.caption_text:
I’m sitting around 18% body fat right now… but it definitely didn’t start here.

There was a long stretch where I looked “fine” to everyone else… but I didn’t feel like myself. And as a girl struggling with hypothyroidism everything felt 10x worse.

My clothes didn’t fit
My mood was all over the place 
My energy was gone
My cravings were out of control
And I kept telling myself I’d get serious “next month”… every single month.

If you’re there right now, I get it and I want to give you a big hug and tell you it gets better! 

What finally worked for me wasn’t some crazy gym plan or extreme diet cutting out carbs and all the things I loved. It was actually really simple… I just started doing the basics over and over again until they were routine. 

✨A Calorie deficit will ALWAYS win if you do it right, 
I stopped guessing. I got honest about my portions and what I was actually eating vs what my body needed & figured out how to eat a little less to lose fat (300-500 calories is all you need to ditch and that could be a specialty coffee or small snack for most people!!) 

💡Strength training
I lift 3–4x a week and did it all from home! These aren’t going to burn a bunch of calories, but it’s about supporting your metabolism and building your shape
This is what actually gives you that toned, lean look
.
👟 Daily steps (8-10k)
This was a huge one for me. Not more workouts… more movement throughout the day.
It adds up more than you think.

🍷 Less alcohol
I didn’t cut it completely, but I became way more intentional.
Better sleep, less bloating, and way more consistent progress.

None of this is extreme.

That’s actually the point.

The difference was doing all of it together… consistently… for long enough to see real change.

Not starting over every Monday. Not waiting for “next month.”

Just finally following through.

If you’re ready to stop saying “I’ll start soon” and actually do it… this is your sign.

Comment READY if you’re starting your own transformation. All send you my free, get toned guide with my workout split a sample meal plan and some tips to help get you started. I want to know where you’re at 🤍

items.caption_text:
3 habits that made losing 45 lbs feel a LOT easier

I used to think weight loss had to feel hard all the time. Like I needed more discipline, more willpower, more motivation.

But the biggest shift for me was making it feel simple and repeatable.

These are the 3 habits that changed everything:

1. Protein at every meal (30–40g)
Once I started actually hitting about 130–150g a day, I wasn’t constantly hungry or thinking about food. It made staying in a calorie deficit way easier without feeling restricted.

2. Walking every single day (10–12k steps)
This was the missing piece for me. Not more workouts… more daily movement. It keeps your calorie burn up without stressing your body.

3. Keeping my meals simple
Same foods, same structure. Protein + veggies + a carb.
Less decision making = way more consistency.

That’s it.

Not a crazy diet. Not a new plan every week. Just habits I could stick to over and over again.

That’s what helped me lose 45 lbs… and actually keep it off.

Follow for more simple, realistic weight loss tips.

items.caption_text:
When I was losing weight, I didn’t overhaul my entire diet. I found a handful of foods that kept me full, hit my protein, and were easy to repeat… and I stuck with them.

These were on repeat.

Eggs + egg whites
Easy way to start the day with protein so I’m not starving by 10am.

Lean ground turkey or chicken
My go to for bowls, wraps, or quick dinners. High protein, super filling.

Greek yogurt or protein shakes
This is what helped me stop reaching for random snacks. Quick, high protein, and actually satisfying.

Salmon
Healthy fats, great for hormones, and keeps me full way longer than a low fat meal.

Potatoes
This one surprises people, but they’re super filling and helped me stay consistent instead of feeling restricted.

I didn’t need fancy recipes.
I just needed foods I could eat on repeat without overthinking it.

That’s what made it sustainable.

Follow for more simple fat loss meals and tips.

items.caption_text:
I’ve been there, because I truly thought I was doing everything right and nothing was happening. I blamed my hypothyroidism, my genetics, my “big bones”🫠 but in reality looking back, I was just doing way too much of the wrong stuff. 

too many rules
too many extremes 
too many “healthy swaps”
overthinking every little thing

so if that’s you right now, stop overcomplicating it, you’re doing too much

here’s how it actually works:

Calories = weight (you MUST be in a deficit to lose fat)
Protein = results (satiety, muscle, metabolism)
Strength training = shape (build muscle, “tone”)
Steps = fat loss (daily burn, low stress)
Sleep = hormone balance (hunger, cortisol, recovery)
Hydration + electrolytes = performance (energy & digestion)
Creatine = strength + brain (power, recovery, focus)
Consistency = results (repeat what works)

that’s it

when I focused on just these, everything started to click

I wasn’t second guessing every meal
my energy felt better
and my body actually started responding again

no extremes, no starting over every Monday, just doing the basics consistently

if you feel stuck, you probably don’t need more information, you need to simplify and stick with it🤍

Comment EAT and I’ll DM you with my list of the ONLY 10 foods you need to lose fat 🤍

items.caption_text:
These are some of those small changes that made a really big difference for me when I first lost 45 pounds, but even more so now having had a baby in my late 30s getting in the best shape of my life.

The one I thought would be the hardest to change was my habit of waking up and going straight to coffee on an empty stomach. And I didn’t realize how much that was working against me.

But changing it actually was way easier than I thought, even as a girl who wakes up at 5 AM and definitely doesn’t want to eat that early.

After having my son, I started just doing a scoop of LSF protein @lsfnutrition in water and drink it quickly, or I’ll add it into my coffee. Super simple.

Here’s why it matters, especially for women:

When you have caffeine first thing on an empty stomach, it can spike cortisol. That can lead to more cravings later, energy crashes, and even make it harder to manage belly fat over time.

Getting protein in first helps stabilize blood sugar, supports your metabolism, and keeps you fuller so you’re not reaching for snacks by 10am.

I also noticed I feel way less shaky, way more focused, and my hunger feels way more controlled throughout the day.

That’s the biggest difference between the “stop this” and “do this” for me.
Not just what you eat… but how you start your day.

It’s such a small shift, but it sets the tone for everything that follows it helps make sure I’m hitting my protein goal, avoid blood sugar crashes, and keep cortisol and check throughout the day.

Comment PROTEIN and I’ll send you the one I use every day. No bloating, no sugar, no gritty texture and actually tastes good 🤍

I also noticed I feel way less shaky, way more focused, and my hunger feels way more controlled throughout the day.

That’s the biggest difference between the “stop this” and “do this” for me.
Not just what you eat… but how you start your day.

It’s such a small shift, but it sets the tone for everything that follows it helps make sure I’m hitting my protein goal, avoid blood sugar crashes, and keep cortisol and check throughout the day.

Comment PROTEIN and I’ll send you the one I use every day. No bloating, no sugar, no gritty texture and actually tastes good 🤍

items.caption_text:
A flatter stomach isn’t JUST about losing fat and I wish I realized that sooner…

For the longest time, I thought I just needed to work out harder or eat less. But a lot of what I was dealing with day to day wasn’t even fat. It was bloating, water retention and my digestion being WAY off…(no, only pooping once a week is NOT normal!) yes I had extra fat, but all this made my stomach look and feel even worse.

These are the habits that actually made the biggest difference for me.

First, walking daily. This one sounds too simple, but it’s huge. Even just getting 8–12k steps helps your digestion move, reduces that “stuck” feeling after meals and naturally helps with fat loss too.

Second, eating high protein meals and healthy fiber. . When I started building my meals around protein instead of snacks or carbs first, I noticed I stayed fuller, snacked less, and my stomach felt way less puffy I also use protein powder 1-2x a day but ONLY this one because it’s formulated to be easy to digest and doesn’t bloat . ** I also eat a kiwi 🥝 every day now to help with pooping. 

Third, supporting digestion with enzymes. I take Bloat Be Gone 2x a day with  bigger meals, especially if I’m eating out or having something heavier. It helps my body actually break down the food instead of feeling heavy and uncomfortable in my stomach after and has herbs that reduce water weight! 

And fourth, hydration with electrolytes. Not just chugging water, but actually adding minerals so your body can use it. This helps reduce that tight, bloated feeling and keeps everything moving. I add a pinch of sea salt 🧂 to my water and it’s a cheap way to get more electrolytes in! 

None of this is extreme. It’s not a detox or a quick fix. But doing these things consistently made my stomach feel flatter, lighter, and just better overall.

If you’ve ever felt like you wake up with a flat stomach and go to bed feeling 5 months pregnant… you know exactly what I mean! 

✨comment BLOAT if you want me to DM you the link Bloat Be Gone enzymes and protein powder  I take!

items.caption_text:
The burger bowl is life in a calorie deficit 🤤 this is the exact grocery list I come back to whenever I’m trying to lose fat. You don’t need it all, but if you stick to things off of this list, you’ll be in great shape 

We’re not shopping for our cravings, we’re shopping for our goals. If you’re trying to lose fat and eat healthier, get this right first, worry about calories later. If you stick to these foods 90% of the time I guarantee you will start to drop weight.

Yes, you can overeat healthy foods, but if you’re hitting that protein goal, it’s gonna be really hard to!

items.caption_text:
This might sound a little blunt, but it’s something I had to learn the hard way during my own weight loss journey.

If you’re always guessing your calories, it’s gonna be almost impossible to lose weight consistently and keep it off.

For years I thought I was eating “pretty healthy.” Smoothies, salads, snacks, granola, nut butter… all the things that seem like the right choice. But when I finally started paying attention to portions, I realized I was eating way more calories than I thought.

You don’t have to track forever. I don’t.
But taking a couple weeks to actually measure and understand what you’re eating can completely change how you approach your meals.

Once I understood that, losing the 45 lbs became a lot more straightforward…. 

If you need help figuring out how much YOU need, comment CALORIES and I’ll send you the super easy method I like to use! 

And make sure to follow here @lovesweatfitness for more realistic weight loss tips, simple meals, and habits that actually work.

items.caption_text:
I used to feel puffy all the time no matter how “healthy” I ate… and for years I thought my body just wouldn’t respond. If you deal with bloating, IBS, or hypothyroidism my me (yes, all the above 🫠)trying to lose fat can feel impossible, but I’m promise it can be way easier than you think.

Now I’m down 45 lbs and maintaining muscle, and I’ve learned that what I eat matters just as much as how much I eat when it comes to staying lean without the bloat.

Right now I’m on week 3 of a mini cut before my birthday, aiming to drop about 4 lbs in 4 weeks while keeping protein around 130g and calories about 1500. I’m also fighting a little cold this week, so workouts are lighter, but I’m sticking to simple meals that keep my digestion happy and help me stay consistent.

Here’s what a day looks like 👇
Breakfast
Egg scramble (2 eggs + 1 cup egg whites), sautéed spinach, 1 slice sourdough
400 calories
38g protein, 30g carbs, 14g fat

Lunch
Lean turkey bowl (5 oz ground turkey, roasted zucchini, cucumber, tomato, ½ cup jasmine rice, a little sauerkraut)
420 calories
38g protein, 35g carbs, 14g fat

Snack
Chocolate peanut butter protein smoothie (1 scoop LSF protein @lsfnutrition , almond milk, ice, cocoa powder, peanut butter powder)
220 calories
26g protein, 12g carbs, 6g fat

Dinner
Salmon plate (5 oz salmon, roasted broccoli, small baked potato, squeeze of lemon)
410 calories
32g protein, 28g carbs, 18g fat
Total: about 1450 calories 130g protein

Nothing fancy. Just meals that keep me full, energized, and way less bloated while still leaning out.

✨If you struggle with bloating while trying to lose fat, comment BELLY and I’ll send you my fave debloating supplements I personally use to support digestion and fat loss

items.caption_text:
This is the MOST underrated hack to help you lose weight. Now be honest, how many hours are you getting a night? 

When I was trying to lose weight, I used to focus only on food and workouts, but I was sleeping like garbage and kept feeling like I was spinning my wheels. But once I started focusing on my sleep too, everything got easier. My cravings went down, my energy went up, and sticking to my calorie deficit didn’t feel like a constant battle.

I aim for 7 hours MINIMUM a night if you’re serious about fat loss. And set a sleep and wake up alarm for the same time every day (yes, even on weekends!) 

One study found that people who slept 5 hours instead of 8 hours lost 55% less fat and more muscle even while eating the same calories🤯

Sleep helps regulate hunger hormones, lowers cortisol, and improves recovery so your workouts actually work for you and you get the results you’re looking for.

A few simple things that help me:
• stop caffeine by early afternoon (ideally like 1pm)
• keep lights low the last hour before bed 
• drink my nighttime hot chocolate that helps me relax and sleep deeper about 30mins before I want to fall asleep 

Comment SLEEP and I’ll send you the yummy hot chocolate powder I make every night that helps me fall asleep fast, without crazy dreams and helps me wake up without that groggy feeling.
